tool_args.label: Annual Report on Variable Rate Debt
tool_args.raw_text: Report to the Finance Committee. Annual Report on Variable Rate Debt. MTA Finance Department April 27, 2026.

payload.title: Annual Report on Variable Rate Debt
payload.description: Annual report on MTA's variable rate and hedged/unhedged variable rate debt portfolio as of April 27, 2026.

Variable Rate and Short-Term Debt Aggregate

payload.scope: variable rate and shorter-term/intermediate debt structures
payload.description: In aggregate, variable rate and shorter-term/intermediate debt structures account for $7.4 billion (15%) of the portfolio

warnings: metric_claim.value_unit is accepted as passthrough, but prefer unit. value_unit has only 9 accepted observations; submit one metric per measurement and use unit for the measurement unit.

payload.description: Variable rate debt accounts for 6% of the portfolio
well within the 25% limit set in the MTA's Variable Rate Debt policy

payload.raw_value_text: 65% of outstanding variable rate bonds are hedged
payload.description: 65% of outstanding variable rate bonds are hedged through payment agreements

payload.description: Average Weighted Monthly Rate for MTA Variable Rate Bonds in 2026 YTD through April 15, 2026

payload.raw_value_text: the average benefit between short-term rates (SIFMA) and long-term rates (MMD) widened to 1.63%

payload.raw_value_text: Variable rate demand bonds ("VRDBs"), $2.2 billion outstanding

payload.description: Tax-exempt term loan with a floating interest rate (SIFMA + fixed spread)
payload.label: CRZ Tolling Program Loan

payload.raw_value_text: supported by a diversified portfolio of 6 banks and 8 remarketing agents
payload.description: 6 banks providing Letters of Credit for VRDBs

payload.raw_value_text: $132 million VRDBs with expiring LOCs for the remainder of 2026

payload.claim_text: MTM of the MTA swap portfolio improved by over 15% over the last year from a negative $86 million
payload.subject: MTA swap portfolio MTM
payload.description: Mark-to-Market values represent the theoretical mid-market termination amounts as of a given valuation date and do not impact capital or operating budgeting

payload.claim_text: Other Short-Term Debt includes $2.98 billion Bond Anticipation Notes, $1.3 billion Fixed Rate Balloon Obligations, and $367 million Term Rate bonds which have a fixed rate for a defined period (until the mandatory tender date) but do not have a fixed rate for the entire life of the bonds
payload.subject: MTA Other Short-Term Debt components

tool_args.label: MTA Finance Department published the report
